procedure_name: AKI (Acute Kidney Injury) / Renal Failure (Dialysis, if required, will be add on package)
specialty: General Medicine/ Pediatric medical management/ General surgery
min_doctor_qualification: The patient should be periodically seen by a nephrologist to decide the dialysis prescription and line of treatment
clinical_indications: Acute Kidney Injury with clinical features such as reduced urine output, dark concentrated urine, swelling of face and limbs, breathlessness
Acute Kidney Injury with investigations showing elevated serum creatinine, electrolyte/acid-base abnormalities, supportive imaging and serological findings
Chronic Kidney Disease with clinical features such as Edema, Hematuria, Proteinuria, renal stone, family history of kidney diseases, unexplained hypertension, Anemia
Chronic Kidney Disease with investigations showing elevated serum creatinine, supportive imaging findings
Abnormalities of kidney structure or function, present for >3 months (for Chronic Kidney Disease)

preauth.label: Pathological Examination (Complete Blood count, Blood urea, Serum Creatinine, GFR, serum electrolytes; monthly for chronic dialysis, quarterly for Serum Iron, ferritin, TIBC, TSAT, SGOT, SGPT, viral markers, calcium, phosphate)

claim.label: Detail discharge Summary (only discharge summary for AKI/Renal Failure)

common_queries: Diagnosis of Acute Kidney Injury or Chronic Kidney disease/End-stage kidney disease not mentioned in clinical notes
Pathological examination reports (CBC, Blood urea, Serum Creatinine, GFR, serum electrolytes) missing or incomplete, or not repeated monthly/quarterly as required for chronic cases
Planned line of treatment not documented
Discharge summary missing or incomplete at claim stage
AKI diagnostic criteria (serum creatinine increase or urine output decrease) not met or documented
CKD diagnostic criteria (abnormalities of kidney structure/function for >3 months) not met or documented

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/ DNB (Medicine); DM/ DNB (Nephrology) or equivalent
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with Haemodialysis only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms and investigations.
A patient with pre-existing CKD may develop AKI
referral_criteria: Initial evaluation of all newly diagnosed cases (CKD)
